steps.speech: At the center of our solar system is the Sun, a massive main-sequence star that contains over 99 percent of the mass in the entire solar system.
Orbiting closest to the Sun are the terrestrial planets: Mercury, Venus, Earth, and Mars. They are relatively small and have solid, rocky surfaces.
Further out are the giants. Jupiter and Saturn are massive gas giants, while Uranus and Neptune are freezing cold ice giants.
